task_completion.description: Mount Volume Alpha to access the first keyfile.
task_completion.visual_evidence: VeraCrypt main window showing 'alpha_entry.hc' mounted to a drive slot, or a file manager window displaying the contents of the mounted Alpha volume.

task_completion.description: Mount Volume Beta using the password and the keyfile extracted from Alpha.
task_completion.visual_evidence: VeraCrypt main window showing 'beta_relay.hc' mounted to a drive slot, or a file manager window displaying the contents of the mounted Beta volume.

task_completion.description: Mount Volume Gamma using the password and the keyfile extracted from Beta.
task_completion.visual_evidence: VeraCrypt main window showing 'gamma_vault.hc' mounted to a drive slot, or a file manager window displaying the contents of the mounted Gamma volume including 'coordinates.csv'.

task_completion.description: Copy 'coordinates.csv' to the specified Documents folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager or terminal showing a file named 'recovered_coordinates.csv' located inside the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

task_completion.description: Dismount all volumes before finishing the task.
task_completion.visual_evidence: VeraCrypt main window visible in the final screenshots showing all drive slots empty, indicating all volumes have been successfully dismounted.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the VeraCrypt application to mount the volumes.
integrity.visual_evidence: VeraCrypt GUI windows, including password and keyfile selection dialogs, should be visible in the trajectory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent copied the file from the mounted volume rather than creating a fake file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a copy-paste action in the file manager from the mounted Gamma volume, or a 'cp' command in the terminal originating from the mount point.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just create standard folders named after the volumes.
integrity.visual_evidence: The volumes must appear as mounted drives in the VeraCrypt interface or system mount points, not just regular directories created via 'mkdir'.
